evals.prompt: Hey, I just installed OpenClaw and ran `openclaw onboard`. I want to connect my Telegram bot — I already have a token from BotFather. What's the exact command to add it, and how do I make sure only I can DM the bot (not random people)?
evals.expected_output: Explains `openclaw channels add --channel telegram --token ...`, sets dmPolicy to pairing or allowlist, mentions BotFather privacy mode
assertions: Response mentions the command `openclaw channels add --channel telegram` or `botToken` config key
Response explains dmPolicy options (pairing or allowlist) to restrict who can DM the bot
Response mentions BotFather privacy mode (/setprivacy) or equivalent access control mechanism

evals.prompt: I'm running OpenClaw on a VPS and want to store my Telegram bot token and gateway auth token as secrets instead of putting them in plain text in openclaw.json. How do I do that? Also I saw something about needing to set gateway.auth.mode explicitly now — what's that about?
evals.expected_output: Explains SecretRef ($secret syntax), gateway.auth.mode breaking change in v2026.3.7, env var resolution
assertions: Response mentions the gateway.auth.mode breaking change introduced in v2026.3.7
Response explains that SecretRefs are resolved from environment variables at runtime

evals.prompt: I paired my iPhone with OpenClaw as a node. Now I want my agent to be able to take a photo from my phone's camera when I ask it to, and also display web pages on the phone screen. What tools does the agent use for this, and are there any limitations I should know about?
evals.expected_output: Explains nodes tool, camera.snap, canvas.present, foreground app requirement, node.invoke surface
assertions: Response mentions `camera.snap` action or `node.invoke` as the mechanism to take a photo
Response mentions `canvas.present` for displaying web pages/HTML on the device
Response mentions the foreground app requirement (canvas/camera require the app to be in the foreground on mobile)
